steps.title: Compare filings and penalties
steps.content: The graph shows self-dealing made up a much smaller share of filings than undistributed income but a large share of total penalties. Average penalty per filing is estimated by comparing each activity's penalty share with its filing share.

steps.title: Infer the higher average
steps.content: Choice A compares to Form 990 organizations, which the graph does not do. Choice B follows because self-dealing has a higher penalty share relative to its filing share than undistributed income. Choice C misstates collective shares. Choice D says no majority of penalties, but undistributed income has about 55 percent. The answer is B.
